# Generated by anthropic (claude-opus-4-6)
# Prompt: Create a GitLab CI pipeline that uses merge request title and description in build notifications and commit messages. This helps track which features are being deployed and generates automatic release notes. Include the MR title in deployment tags.
# Category: cicd_security

stages:
  - validate
  - build
  - test
  - release-notes
  - deploy
  - notify

variables:
  RELEASE_NOTES_FILE: "release_notes.md"
  DEPLOY_TAG_PREFIX: "deploy"

# Validate merge request metadata
validate_mr_metadata:
  stage: validate
  rules:
    - if: '$CI_PIPELINE_SOURCE == "merge_request_event"'
  script:
    - echo "Validating Merge Request metadata..."
    - echo "MR Title - $CI_MERGE_REQUEST_TITLE"
    - echo "MR Description - $CI_MERGE_REQUEST_DESCRIPTION"
    - echo "MR IID - $CI_MERGE_REQUEST_IID"
    - echo "MR Source Branch - $CI_MERGE_REQUEST_SOURCE_BRANCH_NAME"
    - echo "MR Target Branch - $CI_MERGE_REQUEST_TARGET_BRANCH_NAME"
    - |
      if [ -z "$CI_MERGE_REQUEST_TITLE" ]; then
        echo "ERROR: Merge request title is empty. Please provide a meaningful title."
        exit 1
      fi
    - |
      # Enforce conventional commit style in MR title
      if echo "$CI_MERGE_REQUEST_TITLE" | grep -qE "^(feat|fix|chore|docs|refactor|test|perf|ci|style|build|revert)(\(.+\))?:"; then
        echo "MR title follows conventional commit format."
      else
        echo "WARNING: MR title does not follow conventional commit format."
        echo "Expected format: type(scope): description"
        echo "Types: feat, fix, chore, docs, refactor, test, perf, ci, style, build, revert"
      fi
    - |
      # Check MR description is not empty
      if [ -z "$CI_MERGE_REQUEST_DESCRIPTION" ]; then
        echo "WARNING: Merge request description is empty. Consider adding a description for better release notes."
      fi

# Build stage with MR context
build:
  stage: build
  rules:
    - if: '$CI_PIPELINE_SOURCE == "merge_request_event"'
    - if: '$CI_COMMIT_BRANCH == "main"'
  script:
    - echo "Building project..."
    - |
      if [ -n "$CI_MERGE_REQUEST_TITLE" ]; then
        echo "Build triggered by MR !${CI_MERGE_REQUEST_IID}: ${CI_MERGE_REQUEST_TITLE}"
        echo "MR_TITLE=${CI_MERGE_REQUEST_TITLE}" >> build_metadata.env
        echo "MR_IID=${CI_MERGE_REQUEST_IID}" >> build_metadata.env
        echo "MR_DESCRIPTION=${CI_MERGE_REQUEST_DESCRIPTION}" >> build_metadata.env
        echo "MR_AUTHOR=${CI_MERGE_REQUEST_AUTHOR}" >> build_metadata.env
        echo "MR_SOURCE_BRANCH=${CI_MERGE_REQUEST_SOURCE_BRANCH_NAME}" >> build_metadata.env
      else
        echo "Build triggered by direct push to ${CI_COMMIT_BRANCH}"
        echo "COMMIT_TITLE=${CI_COMMIT_TITLE}" >> build_metadata.env
      fi
    - echo "BUILD_TIMESTAMP=$(date -u +%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ)" >> build_metadata.env
    - echo "BUILD_COMMIT=${CI_COMMIT_SHORT_SHA}" >> build_metadata.env
    # Simulated build step
    - echo "Compiling application..."
    - mkdir -p build_output
    - cp build_metadata.env build_output/
  artifacts:
    paths:
      - build_output/
    reports:
      dotenv: build_metadata.env
    expire_in: 1 week

# Test stage
test:
  stage: test
  rules:
    - if: '$CI_PIPELINE_SOURCE == "merge_request_event"'
    - if: '$CI_COMMIT_BRANCH == "main"'
  needs:
    - build
  script:
    - echo "Running tests..."
    - |
      if [ -n "$MR_TITLE" ]; then
        echo "Testing changes from MR !${MR_IID}: ${MR_TITLE}"
      fi
    - echo "All tests passed."

# Generate release notes from MR information
generate_release_notes:
  stage: release-notes
  rules:
    - if: '$CI_COMMIT_BRANCH == "main"'
  script:
    - echo "Generating release notes..."
    - |
      # Create release notes file
      cat > ${RELEASE_NOTES_FILE} <<EOF
      # Release Notes - $(date -u +%Y-%m-%d)

      ## Build Information
      - **Commit:** ${CI_COMMIT_SHORT_SHA}
      - **Branch:** ${CI_COMMIT_BRANCH}
      - **Pipeline:** ${CI_PIPELINE_ID}
      - **Timestamp:** $(date -u +%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ)

      ## Changes
      EOF
    - |
      # Fetch recent merge request information via API
      echo "Fetching recent merged MRs..."
      LAST_TAG=$(git describe --tags --abbrev=0 2>/dev/null || echo "")
      if [ -n "$LAST_TAG" ]; then
        echo "Generating notes since tag: ${LAST_TAG}"
        SINCE_DATE=$(git log -1 --format=%aI ${LAST_TAG})
      else
        echo "No previous tag found. Generating notes for last 7 days."
        SINCE_DATE=$(date -u -d '7 days ago' +%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ 2>/dev/null || date -u -v-7d +%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ)
      fi
    - |
      # Use GitLab API to fetch merged MRs
      MERGED_MRS=$(curl --silent --header "PRIVATE-TOKEN: ${GITLAB_API_TOKEN}" \
        "${CI_API_V4_URL}/projects/${CI_PROJECT_ID}/merge_requests?state=merged&target_branch=main&updated_after=${SINCE_DATE}&per_page=50" 2>/dev/null || echo "[]")

      if [ "$MERGED_MRS" != "[]" ] && [ -n "$MERGED_MRS" ]; then
        echo "" >> ${RELEASE_NOTES_FILE}
        echo "### Merged MRs" >> ${RELEASE_NOTES_FILE}
        echo "" >> ${RELEASE_NOTES_FILE}

        echo "$MERGED_MRS" | python3 -c "
      import sys, json
      try:
          mrs = json.load(sys.stdin)
          features = []
          fixes = []
          others = []
          for mr in mrs:
              title = mr.get('title', 'No title')
              iid = mr.get('iid', '?')
              author = mr.get('author', {}).get('name', 'Unknown')
              description = mr.get('description', '') or ''
              # Truncate description for release notes
              short_desc = description[:200] + '...' if len(description) > 200 else description
              entry = f'- **MR !{iid}**: {title} (by {author})'
              if short_desc:
                  entry += f'\n  > {short_desc}'
              if title.startswith('feat'):
                  features.append(entry)
              elif title.startswith('fix'):
                  fixes.append(entry)
              else:
                  others.append(entry)
          if features:
              print('#### Features')
              print('\n'.join(features))
              print()
          if fixes:
              print('#### Bug Fixes')
              print('\n'.join(fixes))
              print()
          if others:
              print('#### Other Changes')
              print('\n'.join(others))
              print()
      except Exception as e:
          print(f'Error parsing MRs: {e}')
      " >> ${RELEASE_NOTES_FILE}
      else
        echo "No merged MRs found for this period." >> ${RELEASE_NOTES_FILE}
      fi
    - |
      # Also include raw commit messages since last tag
      echo "" >> ${RELEASE_NOTES_FILE}
      echo "### Commits" >> ${RELEASE_NOTES_FILE}
      echo "" >> ${RELEASE_NOTES_FILE}
      if [ -n "$LAST_TAG" ]; then
        git log ${LAST_TAG}..HEAD --pretty=format:"- %h %s (%an)" >> ${RELEASE_NOTES_FILE}
      else
        git log --oneline -20 --pretty=format:"- %h %s (%an)" >> ${RELEASE_NOTES_FILE}
      fi
    - echo ""
    - echo "Release notes generated:"
    - cat ${RELEASE_NOTES_FILE}
  artifacts:
    paths:
      - ${RELEASE_NOTES_FILE}
    expire_in: 1 year

# Deploy with MR title in tag
deploy_staging:
  stage: deploy
  rules:
    - if: '$CI_COMMIT_BRANCH == "main"'
  needs:
    - test
    - generate_release_notes
  environment:
    name: staging
    url: https://staging.example.com
  script:
    - echo "Deploying to staging..."
    - |
      # Create a sanitized tag name from MR title or commit message
      RAW_TITLE="${CI_COMMIT_TITLE}"
      # Sanitize: lowercase, replace spaces and special chars with dashes
      SANITIZED_TITLE=$(echo "$RAW_TITLE" | tr '[:upper:]' '[:lower:]' | sed 's/[^a-z0-9]/-/g' | sed 's/--*/-/g' | sed 's/^-//' | sed 's/-$//' | head -c 60)
      DEPLOY_TAG="${DEPLOY_TAG_PREFIX}/staging/v$(date +%Y%m%d%H%M%S)-${CI_COMMIT_SHORT_SHA}-${SANITIZED_TITLE}"
      echo "Creating deployment tag: ${DEPLOY_TAG}"
    - |
      # Create annotated git tag with MR details in message
      git config user.email "ci@${CI_SERVER_HOST}"
      git config user.name "GitLab CI"

      TAG_MESSAGE="Staging Deployment - $(date -u +%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ)

      Commit: ${CI_COMMIT_SHA}
      Title: ${CI_COMMIT_TITLE}
      Pipeline: ${CI_PIPELINE_URL}
      "

      if [ -n "$CI_MERGE_REQUEST_TITLE" ]; then
        TAG_MESSAGE="${TAG_MESSAGE}
      MR: !${CI_MERGE_REQUEST_IID} - ${CI_MERGE_REQUEST_TITLE}
      MR Author: ${CI_MERGE_REQUEST_AUTHOR}
      MR Description: ${CI_MERGE_REQUEST_DESCRIPTION}
      "
      fi

      git tag -a "${DEPLOY_TAG}" -m "${TAG_MESSAGE}" || echo "Tag creation skipped (might need push permissions)"
    - |
      # Push the tag
      git push "https://gitlab-ci-token:${GITLAB_API_TOKEN}@${CI_SERVER_HOST}/${CI_PROJECT_PATH}.git" "${DEPLOY_TAG}" 2>/dev/null || echo "Tag push skipped (token may not have write access)"
    - echo "Staging deployment complete with tag ${DEPLOY_TAG}"

deploy_production:
  stage: deploy
  rules:
    - if: '$CI_COMMIT_BRANCH == "main"'
      when: manual
  needs:
    - deploy_staging
    - generate_release_notes
  environment:
    name: production
    url: https://example.com
  script:
    - echo "Deploying to production..."
    - |
      # Generate production deployment tag
      PROD_TAG="${DEPLOY_TAG_PREFIX}/production/v$(date +%Y%m%d%H%M%S)-${CI_COMMIT_SHORT_SHA}"
      echo "Creating production deployment tag: ${PROD_TAG}"
    - |
      git config user.email "ci@${CI_SERVER_HOST}"
      git config user.name "GitLab CI"

      # Include release notes in tag message
      TAG_MESSAGE="Production Deployment - $(date -u +%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ)

      Commit: ${CI_COMMIT_SHA}
      Title: ${CI_COMMIT_TITLE}
      Pipeline: ${CI_PIPELINE_URL}

      --- Release Notes ---
      $(cat ${RELEASE_NOTES_FILE} 2>/dev/null || echo 'No release notes available')
      "

      git tag -a "${PROD_TAG}" -m "${TAG_MESSAGE}" || echo "Tag creation skipped"
      git push "https://gitlab-ci-token:${GITLAB_API_TOKEN}@${CI_SERVER_HOST}/${CI_PROJECT_PATH}.git" "${PROD_TAG}" 2>/dev/null || echo "Tag push skipped"
    - |
      # Create a GitLab Release using the API
      echo "Creating GitLab Release..."
      RELEASE_NAME="Release $(date +%Y.%m.%d) - ${CI_COMMIT_SHORT_SHA}"
      RELEASE_DESCRIPTION=$(cat ${RELEASE_NOTES_FILE} 2>/dev/null | python3 -c "import sys,json; print(json.dumps(sys.stdin.read()))" | sed 's/^"//;s/"$//')

      curl --silent --request POST \
        --header "PRIVATE-TOKEN: ${GITLAB_API_TOKEN}" \
        --header "Content-Type: application/json" \
        --data "{
          \"name\": \"${RELEASE_NAME}\",
          \"tag_name\": \"${PROD_TAG}\",
          \"description\": ${RELEASE_DESCRIPTION:-\"No release notes\"},
          \"ref\": \"${CI_COMMIT_SHA}\"
        }" \
        "${CI_API_V4_URL}/projects/${CI_PROJECT_ID}/releases" 2>/dev/null || echo "Release creation skipped"
    - echo "Production deployment complete."

# Send build notifications with MR context
notify_slack_mr:
  stage: notify
  rules:
    - if: '$CI_PIPELINE_SOURCE == "merge_request_event"'
  needs:
    - test
  script:
    - |
      # Construct notification message with MR details
      NOTIFICATION_TITLE="Pipeline #${CI_PIPELINE_ID} for MR !${CI_MERGE_REQUEST_IID}"
      NOTIFICATION_BODY="*MR Title:* ${CI_MERGE_REQUEST_TITLE}
      *Author:* ${CI_MERGE_REQUEST_AUTHOR:-Unknown}
      *Source:* \`${CI_MERGE_REQUEST_SOURCE_BRANCH_NAME}\` → \`${CI_MERGE_REQUEST_TARGET_BRANCH_NAME}\`
      *Status:* ${CI_JOB_STATUS}
      *Pipeline:* ${CI_PIPELINE_URL}
      *MR Link:* ${CI_MERGE_REQUEST_URL:-${CI_PROJECT_URL}/-/merge_requests/${CI_MERGE_REQUEST_IID}}"

      if [ -n "$CI_MERGE_REQUEST_DESCRIPTION" ]; then
        # Truncate description for notification
        SHORT_DESC=$(echo "$CI_MERGE_REQUEST_DESCRIPTION" | head -c 300)
        NOTIFICATION_BODY="${NOTIFICATION_BODY}
      *Description:* ${SHORT_DESC}"
      fi

      echo "Notification content:"
      echo "Title: ${NOTIFICATION_TITLE}"
      echo "Body: ${NOTIFICATION_BODY}"
    - |
      # Send to Slack (if webhook URL is configured)
      if [ -n "$SLACK_WEBHOOK_URL" ]; then
        SLACK_PAYLOAD=$(cat <<EOFSLACK
      {
        "blocks": [
          {
            "type": "header",
            "text": {
              "type": "plain_text",
              "text": "${NOTIFICATION_TITLE}"
            }
          },
          {
            "type": "section",
            "text": {
              "type": "mrkdwn",
              "text": "${NOTIFICATION_BODY}"
            }
          },
          {
            "type": "context",
            "elements": [
              {
                "type": "mrkdwn",
                "text": "Project: ${CI_PROJECT_NAME} | Commit: ${CI_COMMIT_SHORT_SHA}"
              }
            ]
          }
        ]
      }
      EOFSLACK
      )
        curl -X POST -H 'Content-type: application/json' \
          --data "${SLACK_PAYLOAD}" \
          "${SLACK_WEBHOOK_URL}" || echo "Slack notification failed"
        echo "Slack notification sent."
      else
        echo "SLACK_WEBHOOK_URL not configured, skipping Slack notification."
      fi

notify_deployment:
  stage: notify
  rules:
    - if: '$CI_COMMIT_BRANCH == "main"'
  needs:
    - deploy_staging
    - generate_release_notes
  script:
    - |
      echo "Sending deployment notification..."
      DEPLOY_MSG="Deployment to staging completed.
      *Commit:* ${CI_COMMIT_SHORT_SHA} - ${CI_COMMIT_TITLE}
      *Pipeline:* ${CI_PIPELINE_URL}
      *Environment:* staging"

      # Include release notes summary in notification
      if [ -f "${RELEASE_NOTES_FILE}" ]; then
        NOTES_SUMMARY=$(head -30 ${RELEASE_NOTES_FILE})
        DEPLOY_MSG="${DEPLOY_MSG}

      *Release Notes Preview:*
      ${NOTES_SUMMARY}"
      fi

      echo "$DEPLOY_MSG"
    - |
      # Send email notification via GitLab API
      if [ -n "$NOTIFICATION_EMAILS" ] && [ -n "$GITLAB_API_TOKEN" ]; then
        echo "Sending email notifications to: ${NOTIFICATION_EMAILS}"
        # Using GitLab's notification system or custom email integration
        curl --silent --request POST \
          --header "PRIVATE-TOKEN: ${GITLAB_API_TOKEN}" \
          --header "Content-Type: application/json" \
          --data "{
            \"body\": \"Deployment notification: ${CI_COMMIT_TITLE} deployed to staging. Pipeline: ${CI_PIPELINE_URL}\"
          }" \
          "${CI_API_V4_URL}/projects/${CI_PROJECT_ID}/merge_requests/${CI_MERGE_REQUEST_IID}/notes" 2>/dev/null || echo "API notification skipped"
      fi
    - |
      # Send to Microsoft Teams (if webhook configured)
      if [ -n "$TEAMS_WEBHOOK_URL" ]; then
        TEAMS_PAYLOAD="{
          \"@type\": \"MessageCard\",
          \"summary\": \"Deployment Notification\",
          \"themeColor\": \"0076D7\",
          \"title\": \"Staging Deployment - ${CI_COMMIT_SHORT_SHA}\",
          \"sections\": [{
            \"activityTitle\": \"${CI_COMMIT_TITLE}\",
            \"facts\": [
              {\"name\": \"Pipeline\", \"value\": \"#${CI_PIPELINE_ID}\"},
              {\"name\": \"Commit\", \"value\": \"${CI_COMMIT_SHORT_SHA}\"},
              {\"name\": \"Branch\", \"value\": \"${CI_COMMIT_BRANCH}\"}
            ]
          }],
          \"potentialAction\": [{
            \"@type\": \"OpenUri\",
            \"name\": \"View Pipeline\",
            \"targets\": [{\"os\": \"default\", \"uri\": \"${CI_PIPELINE_URL}\"}]
          }]
        }"
        curl -H "Content-Type: application/json" -d "${TEAMS_PAYLOAD}" "${TEAMS_WEBHOOK_URL}" || echo "Teams notification failed"
        echo "Teams notification sent."
      else
        echo "TEAMS_WEBHOOK_URL not configured, skipping Teams notification."
      fi

# Automatic changelog commit on production deploy
update_changelog:
  stage: notify
  rules:
    - if: '$CI_COMMIT_BRANCH == "main"'
      when: manual
  needs:
    - deploy_production
    - generate_release_notes
  script:
    - |
      echo "Updating CHANGELOG.md..."
      git config user.email "ci@${CI_SERVER_HOST}"
      git config user.name "GitLab CI"
      git checkout ${CI_COMMIT_BRANCH}
    - |
      # Prepend new release notes to CHANGELOG
      if [ -f "${RELEASE_NOTES_FILE}" ]; then
        if [ -f "CHANGELOG.md" ]; then
          # Prepend new notes to existing changelog
          cat ${RELEASE_NOTES_FILE} > CHANGELOG_NEW.md
          echo "" >> CHANGELOG_NEW.md
          echo "---" >> CHANGELOG_NEW.md
          echo "" >> CHANGELOG_NEW.md
          cat CHANGELOG.md >> CHANGELOG_NEW.md
          mv CHANGELOG_NEW.md CHANGELOG.md
        else
          cp ${RELEASE_NOTES_FILE} CHANGELOG.md
        fi

        git add CHANGELOG.md
        COMMIT_MSG="docs: update changelog for deployment ${CI_COMMIT_SHORT_SHA}

      Auto-generated changelog from pipeline #${CI_PIPELINE_ID}
      Includes changes from commit ${CI_COMMIT_TITLE}"

        git commit -m "${COMMIT_MSG}"
        git push "https://gitlab-ci-token:${GITLAB_API_TOKEN}@${CI_SERVER_HOST}/${CI_PROJECT_PATH}.git" HEAD:${CI_COMMIT_BRANCH} || echo "Changelog push failed"
        echo "CHANGELOG.md updated successfully."
      else
        echo "No release notes file found. Skipping changelog update."
      fi
